questions.question: Which query returns the names of customers who have placed at least one order?

options.A: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id = ANY (SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id);
options.B: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders);
options.C: SELECT name FROM customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.order_id;
options.D: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT order_id FROM orders);

questions.question: Which query returns the names of customers who have never placed an order?

options.A: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT order_id FROM orders);
options.B: SELECT name FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id WHERE orders.order_id IS NULL;
options.C: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders);
options.D: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE customer_id != (SELECT customer_id FROM orders);

questions.question: Which query returns each customer's name and the total quantity of items they have ordered across all their orders?

options.A: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty) AS total_qty FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.B: SELECT c.name, COUNT(oi.qty) AS total_qty FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.C: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty) AS total_qty FROM customers c JOIN order_items oi ON c.customer_id = oi.item_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.D: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty) AS total_qty FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id;

questions.question: Which query returns the customer name and amount of the single most expensive order?

options.A: SELECT c.name, MAX(o.amount) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;
options.B: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id ORDER BY o.amount DESC;
options.C: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.amount = MAX(o.amount);
options.D: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id ORDER BY o.amount DESC LIMIT 1;

questions.question: Which query returns orders whose amount is above the average order amount?

options.A: SELECT order_id, amount FROM orders WHERE amount > AVG(amount);
options.B: SELECT order_id, amount FROM orders WHERE amount > (SELECT MAX(amount) FROM orders);
options.C: SELECT order_id, amount FROM orders WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders);
options.D: SELECT order_id, amount FROM orders HAVING amount > AVG(amount);

questions.question: Which query returns the distinct countries of customers who have placed at least one order, with no duplicate rows?

options.A: SELECT DISTINCT c.country FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;
options.B: SELECT c.country FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;
options.D: SELECT DISTINCT c.country FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

questions.question: Which query returns the name and order amount for every shipped order?

options.A: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
options.B: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'shipped' WHERE c.name IS NOT NULL;
options.C: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
options.D: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';

questions.question: Which query returns the names of customers who have never placed an order, using a LEFT JOIN?

options.A: SELECT c.name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NOT NULL;
options.B: SELECT c.name FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;
options.C: SELECT c.name FROM orders o LEFT JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;
options.D: SELECT c.name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;

questions.question: Which query finds pairs of customers who live in the same city (without repeating the same pair twice and without pairing a customer with themselves)?

options.A: SELECT a.name, b.name, a.city FROM customers a JOIN customers b ON a.city = b.city;
options.B: SELECT a.name, b.name, a.city FROM customers a JOIN customers b ON a.city = b.city AND a.customer_id = b.customer_id;
options.C: SELECT a.name, b.name, a.city FROM customers a JOIN customers b ON a.city = b.city AND a.customer_id < b.customer_id;
options.D: SELECT a.name, b.name FROM customers a CROSS JOIN customers b WHERE a.city = b.city AND a.customer_id != b.customer_id ORDER BY a.customer_id;

questions.question: Which query counts, per customer, how many orders are 'shipped' and how many are 'pending' — in two separate columns?

options.A: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS shipped, COUNT(*) AS pending FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id, status;
options.B: SELECT customer_id, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'shipped' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS shipped, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS pending FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;
options.C: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(status = 'shipped') AS shipped, COUNT(status = 'pending') AS pending FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;
options.D: SELECT customer_id, CASE WHEN status = 'shipped' THEN COUNT(*) END AS shipped FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;

questions.question: Which query returns the customer name and product name for all shipped orders?

options.A: SELECT c.name, oi.product_name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
options.B: SELECT c.name, oi.product_name FROM customers c JOIN order_items oi ON c.customer_id = oi.item_id JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
options.C: SELECT c.name, oi.product_name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.order_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
options.D: SELECT c.name, oi.product_name FROM customers c, orders o, order_items oi WHERE o.status = 'shipped';

questions.question: Which query returns customer names who have placed more than 1 order?

options.A: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) > 1;
options.B: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE COUNT(o.order_id) > 1 GROUP BY c.name;
options.C: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name HAVING o.order_id > 1;
options.D: SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS total FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id HAVING total > 1;

questions.question: Which query returns the name and amount of the single highest-amount order, using a subquery?

options.A: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.amount = MAX(o.amount);
options.B: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id ORDER BY o.amount DESC LIMIT 1;
options.C: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.amount = (SELECT MAX(amount) FROM orders);
options.D: SELECT c.name, MAX(o.amount) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

questions.question: Which query returns all customers, their orders (if any), and the item count per order — including customers with no orders (showing NULL)?

options.A: SELECT c.name, o.order_id, COUNT(oi.item_id) AS item_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name, o.order_id;
options.B: SELECT c.name, o.order_id, COUNT(oi.item_id) AS item_count FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name, o.order_id;
options.C: SELECT c.name, o.order_id, COUNT(oi.item_id) AS item_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id INNER JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name, o.order_id;
options.D: SELECT c.name, o.order_id, COUNT(*) AS item_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id;

questions.question: Which query returns each customer's name and their total revenue (calculated as qty × unit_price across all their order items)?

options.A: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.B: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN order_items oi ON c.customer_id = oi.item_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.C: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.name;
options.D: SELECT c.name, SUM(oi.qty * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id;
